vitakkasaṅkhārasaṇṭhānaṁ.meaning: the stilling of the thought-conditioning; the formation (and settling) of the conditioning that causes thoughts
vitakkasaṅkhārasaṇṭhānaṁ.summary: This compound names the composition or settling of the saṅkhāra (conditioning) underlying thought.
breakdown: Mental conditioning; the conditioned cause or formation underlying the thought.

vitakkasuttaṁ.meaning: the Vitakka Sutta, the “Discourse on Thought”
vitakkasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun (title of a sutta); neuter compound

vitakkentā.gram: present participle of vitakketi, masculine nominative plural

vitakkeyyātha.meaning: you should think; you might think
vitakkeyyātha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural optative of vitakketi (vi + √takk)

vitiṇṇaparalokassa.meaning: of one who has overcome (given up) the other world; of one who has abandoned concern for the world beyond
breakdown: Crossed over, overcome; removed (past participle of vitarati).
World. paraloka: the next world, the world beyond.

vitiṇṇaparalokassāti.meaning: “of one who has overcome the other world” (with quotation marker)
vitiṇṇaparalokassāti.gram: adjective compound, genitive singular + quotation particle ti
breakdown: Quotation particle (iti); the preceding final vowel lengthens by sandhi (-assa + ti > -assāti).

vittamanusocati.meaning: bewails his wealth; grieves over lost possessions
vittamanusocati.gram: verb phrase (compound verb), 3rd person singular present

breakdown: Root: to find, acquire. vitta: what is acquired — wealth.

vitthārena.meaning: in detail; for expounding fully, at length
vitthārena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular used adverbially
breakdown: Root: to spread. vitthāra: expansion, detail.
Instrumental singular ending: “by way of detail”, in full.
Feminine abstract-noun suffix: the experiencing — joy, gladness.

vittilābhañca.meaning: and the gain of joy; and the attainment of prosperity
vittilābhañca.gram: masculine compound noun, accusative singular + enclitic ca
breakdown: And (the -ñ- is sandhi: lābhaṁ + ca > lābhañca).

vivajjaye.meaning: should shun, should avoid, should keep away from
vivajjaye.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of vivajjeti (vi + √vajj)

breakdown: Root: to cover. vi + √var: to uncover, open.

vivareyya.meaning: would get uncovered; should open, would reveal
vivareyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of vivarati
breakdown: Root: to cover; vivarati, to uncover.

vivekamanubrūhayaṁ.meaning: cultivating seclusion; devoting oneself to solitude
vivekamanubrūhayaṁ.gram: present participle phrase, masculine nominative singular (vivekaṁ + anubrūhayaṁ)
breakdown: Seclusion, solitude, detachment (accusative vivekaṁ; the -m- before a- is sandhi).
Cultivating, fostering, devoting oneself to (anu + √brūh, to increase).

vivekanissitaṁ.meaning: based on seclusion; given to detachment
breakdown: Based on, depending on, given to (past participle of nissayati).

vivicceva.meaning: quite secluded from; having become separated, indeed
vivicceva.summary: A sandhi fusion (vivicca + eva) familiar from the jhāna formula: “quite secluded from sense pleasures...”.
vivicceva.gram: gerund of viviccati + emphatic particle eva (sandhi fusion)
breakdown: Having separated, having secluded oneself (gerund of viviccati; vi + √vic).
Emphatic particle: just, indeed (vivicca + eva > vivicceva).

viññugarahitā.meaning: blamed by the wise; censured by intelligent people
viññugarahitā.gram: adjective compound, feminine nominative singular or masculine plural

viññuppasatthā.gram: adjective compound, feminine nominative singular or masculine plural
breakdown: Praised (past participle of pasaṁsati; the pp doubling is junction doubling).
Root: to know. Causative ñāpe: to make known.
Adjectival/action suffix: that which makes known, instructive.

viññāpetukāmassa.meaning: of one desiring to make understood; of one wishing to inform
breakdown: To make known, to give to understand (infinitive of viññāpeti).

viññātasārāni.meaning: whose essence is understood; things whose innermost meaning has been perceived

viññū.meaning: the wise; a wise, intelligent person
viññū.gram: adjective used as noun, from vijānāti (vi + √ñā)
breakdown: Agent suffix: one who knows — the wise.

viññū the.meaning: viññū: the wise; a wise person
viññū the.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply the word viññū, “the wise”.
breakdown: The wise, an intelligent person (vi + √ñā + ū).

viññūhi.meaning: by the wise; by any intelligent person
viññūhi.gram: adjective used as noun, instrumental plural of viññū

vodānapakkhaṁ.meaning: on the side of purity; the faction of purification (conducing to it)
breakdown: Side, party, faction; that which conduces to.
Causing to be cleansed, purification (causative action noun related to vodāna).

voharati.meaning: expresses, defines; decides; uses (a term), designates
breakdown: Prefixes contracted to vo-: apart + down.
Root: to carry. voharati: to use in speech, express, designate; also to do business, decide.

vohārasamucchedo.meaning: the giving up of business; the complete abolishing of trade dealings
breakdown: Complete cutting off, giving up, abolishing (saṁ + ud + √chid).

vohārasamucchedāya.meaning: for the giving up of business; for the abolishing of trade dealings

vohāro.meaning: common use, ordinary usage; current designation; business, trade
breakdown: Root: to carry. vohāra: usage, current expression; also trade, dealings.

voropesiṁ.meaning: I deprived (of life); I took away
voropesiṁ.gram: verb, 1st person singular aorist of voropeti (vi + ava + √ruh, causative)
breakdown: Prefixes contracted to vo-: down, away.
Root; causative rope: to take down. voropeti: to deprive, take away (especially life).

voropeyya.meaning: would deprive of; should take away (life)
voropeyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of voropeti
breakdown: Prefixes contracted to vo-: down, away.
Root; causative rope. voropeti: to deprive, take away.

vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.meaning: ripening in relinquishment; maturing in letting go and handing over
breakdown: Ripening into, maturing towards; handing over.

vuccati.meaning: is called, is said; is spoken of
vuccati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present passive of vacati (√vac)
breakdown: Root: to speak. Passive stem vucca: to be said, to be called.

vuccatānanda.meaning: is called, Ānanda; it is said, Ānanda
vuccatānanda.gram: verb (passive, 3rd person singular) + vocative, sandhi fusion
breakdown: Is called, is said (passive of vacati/vatti).
Vocative: Ānanda (the Buddha's attendant). Sandhi: vuccati + ānanda > vuccatānanda.

vusitavanto.meaning: having fulfilled, accomplished (the holy life); one who has lived (the life)
breakdown: Fulfilled, lived, accomplished (past participle of vivasati; vi + √vas, to dwell).
Possessive suffix: one possessing — one who has fulfilled.

vusitavā.meaning: one who has fulfilled, perfected (the holy life)
breakdown: Nominative singular of the possessive suffix -vant.

vusitaṁ.meaning: fulfilled, lived (as in “the holy life has been lived”)
vusitaṁ.gram: past participle of vivasati (vi + √vas), neuter nominative/accusative singular
breakdown: Root: to dwell, live. vusita: lived through, fulfilled.

vuttappakārena.meaning: in the manner that has been said; by the explanation already stated
vuttappakārena.summary: In context this refers to the conduct and practice of the bhikkhus and bhikkhunīs as depicted by Phussa Thera in his verses.
breakdown: What has been said (past participle of vatti/vuccati).
Manner, way; explanation (the pp doubling is junction doubling).
Instrumental singular ending: by, in the manner of.

vuttassevatthassa.meaning: of the very meaning that was spoken; indeed of the sense already stated
vuttassevatthassa.gram: sandhi phrase, genitive singular (vuttassa + eva + atthassa)
breakdown: Said, spoken (past participle; here genitive vuttassa).

vuttañhetaṁ.meaning: for this was said; this indeed has been spoken
vuttañhetaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: past participle + particle + pronoun (vuttaṁ + hi + etaṁ)
breakdown: For, indeed (the -ñh- is sandhi: vuttaṁ + hi).

vuttā.meaning: said, spoken; it is said (of things stated)
vuttā.gram: past participle of vuccati/vatti, feminine singular or masculine plural
breakdown: Root: to speak. vutta: said, spoken.
Feminine nominative singular or masculine nominative plural ending.

vuttā it is.meaning: vuttā: it is said; said, spoken
vuttā it is.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply the word vuttā, “(it is) said”.

vuṭṭhito.meaning: arisen from, emerged from (e.g. from seclusion or meditation)
vuṭṭhito.gram: past participle of vuṭṭhāti, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root: to stand. vuṭṭhāti: to rise out of, emerge.

vuṭṭhā.gram: past participle of vasati (√vas, to dwell); also spelled vutthā
breakdown: Past participle suffix; vas + ta > vuṭṭha/vuttha, “dwelt, spent”.

vuṭṭhā/vutthā.summary: A glossary entry giving both spellings of the same past participle of vasati.
vuṭṭhā/vutthā.gram: past participle of vasati (√vas), variant spellings
breakdown: Past participle suffix; the cluster appears as either -ṭṭh- or -tth-.
